evidences.title: کاندیدای جدید حرب مستقل در منطقه Bastwell & Daisyfield خانم فازیلا ...
evidences.content: 🚨 معرفی فازیلا پاتل؛ کاندیدای جدید حرب مستقل در منطقه Bastwell & Daisyfield خانم فازیلا پاتل (Fajila Patel) یکی از کاندیداهای انتخابات شورای محلی در منطقه دارون، لنکشایر است که در حال حاضر به‌عنوان یک کاندیدای مستقل در حوزه Blackburn حضور دارد. 🔹 نکته قابل توجه اینجاست که او پیش‌تر، تا همین سال ۲۰۲۲، یکی از کاندیداهای حزب محافظه‌کار (Conservative) بوده است. و ویدیویی نیز برای تبلیغ خود تنظیم کرده بود (در پست منتشز شده) 🔹 اکنون،خانم پاتل مسیر خود را تغییر داده و به‌صورت مستقل برای کرسی شورای منطقه Bastwell & Daisyfield در شورای Blackburn with Darwen نامزد شده است. 📊 او در انتخابات محلی که قرار است در تاریخ ۷ می ۲۰۲۶ برگزار می شود قرار است شرکت کند. او همچنان از مردم شهر درخواست کرده است که به او رای دهند. [...] ویدیو او توجه بسیاری از رای‌دهندگان و تحلیل‌گران محلی را به خود جلب کرده و می‌تواند بر نتایج انتخابات این منطقه تأثیرگذار باشد. 📣 صفحه ایرانیان یوکی سوشال 👤 ادمین: سلطان #IranianUK #UKPolitics #Blackburn #LocalElections #ایرانیان\_یوکی\_سوشال [...] soheila_painting's profile picture soheila\_painting This must be a joke Reply f_kdk's profile picture f\_kdk 🤢🤢🤢🤢🤢🤬🤬🤬😡😡😡😡😡😡😡 Reply kakeroi's profile picture kakeroi من ریدم تو کانادا با این قوانینش Reply zaraaghm's profile pict...

evidences.title: Blackburn council candidate speaks out over absurd claims
evidences.content: A council candidate has spoken out after fake graphics were shared which claimed she had ‘fallen out with her husband’. Fajila Patel is standing as an Independent in the forthcoming council elections in the Daisyfield and Bastwell ward. She is the wife of Little Harwood councillor Tiger Patel. It is not the first time she has stood as a candidate. _ Long-standing Labour councillor and former mayor Parwaiz Akhtar condemned the actions. He said: "It is a disgusting poster and obviously Labour has nothing to do with it. I stand in solidarity with Fajila and her husband Altaf. "While I might not agree with her politics I respect her right to stand as a candidate." Shakeel Choudhry is standing as the 4BWD Independent Candidate in the same ward. It is his first campaign. [...] ### Business ### Crime ### What's On Local Events Features Exhibitions Showbiz ### Traffic ### Notices Bereavement Guide ### Nostalgia ### Archive Subscribe Digital Edition Puzzles Public notices Newsletters Jobs Book an ad Cars Homes Local listings Local info News Sport Rovers Clarets Stanley Business Crime What's On Traffic Notices Nostalgia Archive Local council candidate Fajjla Patel has responded over...

evidences.title: Tory chair in Blackburn hits out over false candidate claims
evidences.content: Political leaders in Blackburn have spoken out against false content being shared against a female council candidate. Fajila Patel is standing as an Independent in the forthcoming council elections in the Daisyfield and Bastwell ward. She is the wife of Little Harwood councillor Altaf 'Tiger' Patel, who was first elected as a Conservative but now sits as an independent. []( Earlier this week, a graphic was shared on WhatsApp that included a picture of Mrs Patel and claimed her husband was “splitting votes” and had “sold her to the Labour Party”. It also told people “not to trust him or Labour” and alleged Reform UK was being used as an excuse. There have been suggestions as to who may have produced or shared the content. [...] “All local political leaders must unite in condemnation of this type of campaigning,and the perpetrators must be brought to justice.” He said: “We find it interesting that when Fajila stood as a Conservative Party candidate her opponents did not see fit to resort to abusing her like this, but feel emboldened to abuse her as an Independent. “It is easy to simply blame the Returning Officer and the elections team for not policing elections; but this is not t...

evidences.title: Guidance for Candidates and Agents at local government elections ...
evidences.content: Each nomination form needs to be signed (subscribed) by two electors, who are on the local government electoral register in the ward you are planning to contest. The electors must be of voting age by polling day and be on the register that is in force on the last day for publication of notice of election.1, Local Elections (Principal Areas) (England and Wales) Rules 2006 (LEPAR 2006)") Each elector may not subscribe more nomination forms than there are vacancies.2, LEPAR 2006") For example, if there are two vacancies in a ward, an elector may subscribe up to two candidates' nomination forms in that ward; if three candidates submit nomination forms subscribed by the same person, the two that were submitted first will be accepted, but the third will be invalid. [...] 1. Schedule 2, Rule 13, Local Elections (Principal Areas) (England and Wales) Rules 2006 ↩ Back to content at footnote 1 Last updated: 20 August 2025 ## Being validly nominated in more than one ward If you are validly nominated in more than one ward within the same local authority, you must withdraw from all wards but one before the deadline for withdrawals (i.e. by 4pm, 19 working days before the poll).
